title: Drop at your own peril: Teams pay heavy price for dropped catches
content: Dropped catches proved costly in IPL 2026, impacting multiple matches. KL Rahul and Shreyas Iyer capitalized on lifelines, leading to massive scores and altered game outcomes. Fielding errors by Punjab Kings and Rajasthan Royals, including dropped chances by Karun Nair and Aniket Verma, significantly influenced the results of high-scoring thrillers.

title: 'Why are you fighting? You're 80': SC issues notice in Sunjay Kapur estate dispute, urges meditation
content: The Supreme Court has issued a notice to Priya Sachdev Kapur and 22 others in an estate dispute involving late businessman Sanjay Kapur. The court suggested mediation to resolve the prolonged family litigation, emphasizing an amicable solution. Rani Kapur's plea seeks to restrain respondents from interfering with the estate and its assets, citing concerns over potential dissipation.

title: China to Mark Zuckerberg's Meta: You cannot buy this company
content: China has blocked Meta's acquisition of AI startup Manus, citing national security concerns and potential technology leakage. This move follows reports of Beijing restricting American capital in its AI sector. The decision stems from Manus's "general AI agent" technology, developed in China but moved to Singapore, raising fears of intellectual property transfer to US firms.

title: How Trump sanctions on China’s pvt refiner may have collateral damage beyond oil
content: Until now, the United States had largely focused its efforts on smaller Chinese companies and facilities in an attempt to curb Iran’s oil revenues, partly to avoid broader economic and diplomatic consequences.

title: 'Vaibhav Sooryavanshi probably using AI chip in bat': 15-year-old told to be sent to 'lab'
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is electrifying the cricket world with his fearless batting. Following a record-breaking 175 in the U19 World Cup final, he's now dominating IPL 2026, recently hitting a 37-ball century. His explosive performances have even led to humorous suggestions of him using an AI chip in his bat, highlighting his extraordinary talent.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 mn
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection day 40: Ranveer Singh film eyes 'Pushpa 2’s all-time domestic record
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge', directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, Sanjay Dutt, Arjun Rampal, R. Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi and Sara Arjun, is on the verge of becoming the second highest-grossing Indian film of all time globally, with a worldwide gross of Rs. 1,777.52 crore, just Rs. 10.54 crore away from surpassing 'Baahubali 2: The Conclusion's Rs. 1,788.06 crore record. On Day 40, the film's India net stands at Rs. 1,130.69 crore, approximately Rs. 103 crore away from 'Pushpa 2: The Rule's all-time domestic record of Rs. 1,234.1 crore.

title: TMC MP Mitali Bag’s car vandalised; party blames BJP, alleges 'attempt on her life'
content: Trinamool Congress alleged that BJP "goons" attacked Arambag MP Mitali Bag's car, vandalizing it and causing her injuries. The party accused Union Home Minister Amit Shah of ordering the attack, calling it an attempt on the MP's life. The incident occurred on the last day of campaigning for the second phase of West Bengal Assembly polls.

title: Virat Kohli on the brink of history, only 11 runs away from...
content: Virat Kohli is just 11 runs away from becoming the first player to reach 9000 IPL runs ahead of Delhi Capitals clash. With 328 runs this season, he continues his remarkable consistency. Dinesh Karthik praised Kohli’s ability to reinvent himself and his disciplined preparation, calling it a learning experience for younger players.

title: OpenAI CEO is literally losing sleep over tech that he says will ‘collapse’ the economy
content: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman sparked debate with predictions of economic collapse post-AGI due to mass unemployment.  Ironically, he's adopting polyphasic sleep, claiming GPT-5.5's advancements are too compelling to miss.  This comes as OpenAI launched GPT-5.5, boasting enhanced reasoning and coding, outperforming rivals.

title: American Mom’s India Lessons: Kristen Fischer’s viral list reshapes daily life
content: In a charmingly candid Instagram post, Kristen Fischer, an American mother, shares the delightful lessons she's learned while living in India. Her viral insights redefine common terms like 'spicy,' 'five minutes,' and personal space, weaving an entertaining tapestry of cultural revelations.

title: Pregnancy changes everything: Why iron deficiency is not the only thing women need to fix
content: Pregnancy significantly alters a woman's body, demanding continuous adjustments to support new life. While iron and folic acid are crucial, a broader spectrum of micronutrients like B vitamins, calcium, vitamin D, magnesium, zinc, copper, and DHA are vital for maternal and fetal health. Addressing these nutritional needs holistically ensures healthier pregnancies and better long-term outcomes.

title: Gold price today: Yellow metal slips; check 24K, 22K city-wise rates
content: Gold prices saw a dip in futures trade on Monday, influenced by weaker global cues and subdued spot demand. While futures declined, retail prices across major Indian cities presented a mixed picture, with some experiencing slight increases and others remaining steady. International gold futures also edged lower, reflecting cautious global trading sentiment.

title: Engine on fire, exit jammed, botched evacuation: How the SWISS Delhi–Zurich flight horror unfolded
content: A Swiss International Air Lines flight aborted take-off at Delhi airport due to an engine fire, leading to a chaotic evacuation. A senior executive described the incident as "complete mismanagement" on the ground, citing issues with emergency response, passenger accounting, and hotel arrangements. The airline crew, however, was praised for their handling of the situation onboard.

title: West Bengal polls: Wafer-thin LS margin gives BJP heart in TMC lair
content: Rashbehari's election heats up as Trinamool's Debasish Kumar faces a strong challenge from BJP's Swapan Dasgupta. Despite Kumar's local roots and past wins, a narrow LS lead for Trinamool in the area signals a tight contest. Both candidates are actively campaigning, addressing issues from slum development to alleged corruption and extortion.

title: Brutal selloff: Infosys loses over Rs 2 lakh cr, slips out of top 10 most valued firms
content: Infosys faces investor pressure, with its market value dropping significantly despite strong March quarter results. A subdued FY27 revenue growth forecast and industry-wide shifts towards cost efficiency and AI-driven productivity are impacting its outlook. While AI presents new opportunities, it also pressures core business margins.

title: 'Abuse of cricket equipment': Angkrish Raghuvanshi fined by IPL
content: Angkrish Raghuvanshi was fined 20% of his match fee and given a demerit point for breaching the IPL Code of Conduct during Kolkata Knight Riders’ clash with Lucknow Super Giants. He reacted angrily after an obstructing-the-field dismissal. Despite the incident, KKR won a Super Over thriller, thanks to Rinku Singh’s unbeaten 83.

title: Maharashtra MSBSHSE HSC result this week amid protest concerns
content: Maharashtra State Board officials have assured that the MSBSHSE HSC results will be announced within the week, dismissing concerns about delays due to employee protests. Authorities confirmed that result preparation and verification are progressing on schedule. Anxiety had risen among students following media reports of disruptions, but the board reiterated there would be no impact. Results will be available online and via DigiLocker, with uploads already in the final stage of completion.

title: BLA claims 27 attacks in Balochistan in 10 days, says 42 Pakistani soldiers killed
content: The Baloch Liberation Army claims to have conducted 27 operations in Balochistan, resulting in 42 Pakistani military personnel killed and one captured.  Attacks included IED blasts, ambushes, and raids targeting security forces and pro-government individuals. The group also reported three of its fighters died. This follows a recent deadly attack on a Pakistan Coast Guard vessel.

title: India-New Zealand FTA signed: Duty on 100% Indian exports down to zero; top points to know
content: India-New Zealand FTA: It aims to double bilateral trade, provide Indian exporters duty-free access to the New Zealand market, and attract investments worth $20 billion over the next 15 years.

title: 'Watching Virat Kohli cry was emotional': Krunal Pandya opens up
content: After an 18-year wait, Royal Challengers Bengaluru clinched their maiden IPL title, defeating Punjab Kings by six runs. All-rounder Krunal Pandya expressed immense emotion, dedicating the historic victory to the passionate RCB fans and long-serving player Virat Kohli, who was visibly moved by the triumph.

title: No Indian yet in Esports Chess World Cup 2026: How can it change?
content: Magnus Carlsen won the Chess.com Open, while Denis Lazavik beat Nihal Sarin, leaving no Indian qualified yet for the Esports World Cup 2026, as of yet. Extra qualification spots don’t pass down, increasing pressure on Sarin. He remains India’s one of the besthopes, with chances through circuit rankings and qualifiers in the evolving esports chess format.

title: 'Good bye tukde-tukde INDI alliance': Kiren Rijiju on Raghav Chadha, 6 AAP MPs joining BJP
content: Union Minister Kiren Rijiju welcomed seven Rajya Sabha MPs who defected from AAP to BJP, praising their parliamentary conduct. The move significantly boosts BJP's strength in the Rajya Sabha, while AAP has petitioned for the disqualification of the defecting members.

title: DGCA grants licence to India’s first seaplane airline; service to start in Lakshadweep
content: India is set to relaunch seaplane services with SkyHop Aviation receiving its operator's license. The company will initially connect five Lakshadweep islands and the mainland using a 19-seater aircraft. This initiative, supported by government incentives and a modified De Havilland Canada DHC-6 Twin Otter, aims to improve regional connectivity and tourism.

title: One trick at a time, Krunal Pandya shows why skill, not match-ups, matters
content: Krunal Pandya has stood out for Royal Challengers Bengaluru in IPL 2026 by adapting brilliantly in a batter-dominated game. With clever variations, bouncers, and sharp tactics, he has handled all match situations. Despite higher economy, his fearless approach and innovation have impressed, with Dinesh Karthik calling him a “trend-setter” redefining finger-spin. Krunal now aims for an India comeback.

title: Parenting shift that makes kids say 'yes' more often; best time to use it
content: Expert unveils a simple parenting trick that empowers children by offering them two acceptable choices, both leading to the desired outcome. This technique fosters independence in kids and reduces daily battles over routines like mornings and bedtime. Parents can effectively use this to encourage cooperation and build confidence in their children.

title: 7 ex-AAP MPs formally join BJP; NDA strength in RS rises to 148, Kejriwal's party down to 3
content: Seven Aam Aadmi Party Rajya Sabha MPs have officially joined the Bharatiya Janata Party, bolstering the NDA's numbers in the Upper House. This move, facilitated by the anti-defection law's provision for two-thirds party mergers, sees the BJP's strength increase significantly. The party anticipates further gains by year-end, nearing a crucial two-thirds majority.

title: Mamata's counter to PM Modi’s 'jhalmuri' break: A visit to local market ahead of Phase 2 polls
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee hit the streets of Kolkata, visiting a local market to connect with vendors and shoppers. She discussed prices of essentials and assured continued state support. This move comes amid a political battle over cultural identity, with Banerjee also criticizing Prime Minister Modi's recent outreach events.

title: 'Bhaiya nahi, brother’: Rinku's family call with Narine steals show - Watch
content: Rinku Singh starred in Kolkata Knight Riders’s thrilling win over Lucknow Super Giants, scoring an unbeaten 83 and taking four catches. Sunil Narine sealed the Super Over by conceding just one run. After the match, a viral video showed Rinku correcting his sister from “bhaiya” to “brother” while praising Narine, leaving everyone laughing.

title: Comfort push: Shatabdi, Jan Shatabdi trains set for nationwide revamp
content: In a communication to all zones, the Railway Board has directed them to conduct detailed inspections of train rakes and prepare time-bound action plans to address deficiencies in passenger amenities.

title: 'I think my mom doesn’t love me': What this 4-year-old reveals will break you
content: A heartbreaking clip from a Korean reality show features a 4-year-old boy expressing profound loneliness and the belief that his mother doesn't love him. The article emphasizes how children absorb emotional cues, leading to lasting beliefs about their worth.

title: Petrol, diesel prices today: Rates steady despite global oil surge - check city-wise list
content: Fuel prices held steady across major Indian cities on Monday, defying volatile global crude oil markets driven by Middle East tensions. Despite Brent crude exceeding $106 per barrel, domestic petrol and diesel rates remained unchanged. This stability, however, is precarious, with potential hikes looming if international oil prices continue their ascent.

title: 'Hope of getting justice shattered': Kejriwal refuses to appear before court, writes to HC judge
content: Former Delhi CM Arvind Kejriwal has refused to appear before the Delhi High Court in the excise policy case, citing a loss of hope for justice. He stated his decision to follow Mahatma Gandhi's path of Satyagraha and will appeal the order in the Supreme Court.

title: Raghav Chadha addresses backlash on joining BJP in new video, cites ‘toxic work environment’ in AAP
content: Raghav Chadha explained his departure from the Aam Aadmi Party, citing a toxic work environment and corruption within the organization. He stated that he felt like the 'right man, but in the wrong party,' and that seven MPs have decided to leave. Chadha asserted the decision was driven by disappointment, not fear, and pledged to continue public service.

title: MBA student raped after grocery delivery; 67-year-old shopkeeper arrested
content: A 67-year-old grocery shop owner faces charges of rape after allegedly assaulting an MBA student in her Bhubaneswar rented room. The incident occurred when he delivered grocery items. The student, who was alone, was attacked after she went to fetch water. The accused was arrested and remanded in judicial custody.

title: IPL security scare: 240 CCTV cameras sabotaged during RCB vs GT match
content: Over 240 CCTV cameras at M Chinnaswamy Stadium were rendered non-functional on April 24. This disruption affected security surveillance during the IPL match between Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Gujarat Titans. Two individuals working for a sub-vendor were apprehended. They allegedly damaged Network Video Recorder systems and fibre connections. The accused claimed non-payment of dues as the reason for their actions.

title: Price prediction: Will gold prices continue to be volatile? Check outlook for this week
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices will closely track movements on the rate decisions by several central banks, including the US Federal Reserve, this week, says Manav Modi, Senior Analyst, Commodity Research at Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd.

title: Pant told to give up LSG captaincy, Ex-India cricketer names replacement
content: Rishabh Pant’s struggles as Lucknow Super Giants captain continued after a fifth straight loss, with poor home results and 14 defeats in 22 games. Former Indian player Wasim Jaffer suggested appointing Aiden Markram as captain, saying leadership pressure is affecting Pant’s batting and that he should focus solely on playing.

title: Jobless man kills dietician wife, seals body in cement box; letter sent to son reveals chilling plot
content: A dietician was murdered by her husband, who then concealed her body in a cement-filled wooden box at their old residence. The accused, Vishal Salvi, confessed to the crime in a letter sent by his son, citing domestic discord and his prolonged unemployment as potential motives. Police discovered the decomposed body after the confession.

title: 'Killers' of the womb: A son obsession
content: Deep-rooted son preference fuels sex-selective abortions in India, leading to millions of fewer female births by 2030. Despite legal bans, illegal networks facilitate prenatal sex determination and terminations, driven by cultural pressures and financial anxieties. While some areas show slow improvement in sex ratios, demand for sons persists.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Policeman stabbed at polling booth in Poompuhar constituency; ex-serviceman arrested
content: Preliminary inquiry revealed that the suspect allegedly harboured hostility towards police personnel and had been involved in a similar assault on a policeman a few years ago

title: Supreme Court takes suo motu note of assault on woman advocate, issues directions
content: The Supreme Court directed the Delhi Police Commissioner to hand over the probe to a senior police person, preferably a woman officer in the rank of ACP or DCP

title: Andhra Pradesh fuel crisis: Naidu orders three-hourly reports; officials deny shortage
content: District authorities denied any shortage and urged the public not to panic-buy, even as queues built up across Visakhapatnam, Anantapur and Rayalaseema

title: Three killed as motorbike hits pedestrian on Mumbai’s Marine Drive
content: The accident occurred at 5.40 A.M. at the Parsi Gymkhana junction signal on N S Road, when the pedestrian was crossing the road, an official said.

title: India, New Zealand ink Free Trade Agreement
content: The pact was signed by Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal and visiting New Zealand’s Trade and Investment Minister Todd McClay

title: Elderly man beaten to death by co-passenger on moving bus in Chennai; youth arrested
content: The accused, a 25-year-old man, had an argument with the elderly passenger over the route details, and assaulted him, causing his death

title: Central forces to stay in West Bengal for seven more days after polls, says Amit Shah
content: The Union Home Minsiter was addressing a gathering after leading a road show in Behala, where voting will take place in the second and final phase on April 29

title: Ladakh L-G Saxena approves creation of 5 new districts
content: With the creation of five new districts — Nubra, Sham, Changthang, Zanskar and Drass — Ladakh will now have seven districts

title: West Bengal elections 2026: Visva Bharati teachers write to Vice-Chancellor, flag ‘extremely stressful’ poll duties
content: Several faculty members of the Central University said they were under “extreme mental and physical stress” due to continuous deployment for poll-related work across districts

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Karur stampede yet to fade from memory of  Velusamypuram voters
content: Minutes past 2 p.m. Thursday, booth number 15 at the Panchayat Union Middle School, located a few yards away from the scene of the stampede, registered 81.31% polling, indicating that the tragedy has in no way dispirited the enthusiasm of the voters

title: Kerala faces hot, humid weather as summer showers remain scanty
content: According to 24-hour data by IMD at 8.30 am on Monday, Palakkad recorded the highest temperature, with the maximum temperature registering 40.1 degrees Celsius. It was followed by Punalur in Kollam, which recorded 39.5 degrees Celsius

title: How an expecting mother’s booster shots can protect her newborn from a host of diseases
content: The Tdap vaccine, Covid-19 and flu shots and the RSV vaccine are some of the important immunisations that expecting mothers should discuss with their health practitioners

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Residents near Katpadi boycott polls after relocation of polling booth
content: Citing relocation of the booth to a farther distance from the colony and a lack of civic amenities in the area, residents chose not to vote

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Polling booth agent arrested for damaging EVM in Tiruvannamalai
content: The damaged EVM was replaced with another machine to continue polling, which was delayed by around 15 minutes

title: West Bengal polls 2026: What Trinamool, BJP, Congress, Left promise in their manifestos
content: The BJP promises an aid of ₹3,000 to unemployed youths — double of Trinamool’s offer — as women’s safety takes centrestage in poll manifestos of major parties

title: Naidu sets ₹4.60-a-unit power purchase target for Andhra Pradesh by year-end
content: Naidu officials to bring down the State’s average power purchase cost to ₹4.60 a unit by the end of the year, and asked that more than half the State’s power generation come from renewable sources by 2028-29

title: CUSAT student death: inquiry reveals no lapses on part of authorities
content: Probe finds that the Head of his department had signed his request for condonation of attendance shortage

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Polling booths in some gated communities across Chennai help boost turnout
content: Group housing societies and large residential buildings in southern fringes like Navalur, Egattur, and Sholinganallur missed out on such faclilities despite applying for it

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Model booths in Tiruchi impress voters across age groups
content: Persons with disabilities and senior citizens were allowed to enter the booth on priority to ensure that they were able to vote comfortably.

title: Karnataka to launch scheme to let farmers buy milch cattle at subsidised prices
content: For the first time in Karnataka, a free anti-foot-and-mouth disease vaccination programme will be launched in all districts

title: TMC MP alleges attack on car by BJP; EC seeks report
content: In a live video, a sobbing Mithali Bag alleged that when her vehicle was passing by the election office of the BJP candidate in Hooghly, miscreants aided by the BJP threw stones at her vehicle, smashing its windows

title: Assembly elections 2026 LIVE: Central forces to stay for seven more days after Bengal polls, even if BJP comes to power, says Amit Shah
content: "Brothers and sisters, go and vote on the 29th, do not worry about the goons. The EC has made extensive security arrangements,” Union Minister Amit Shah said

title: ‘Raghu Rai didn’t just photograph India, he invented a way of seeing it’: Rohit Chawla
content: Goa-based photographer Rohit Chawla remembers how Raghu Rai had little patience for the banal or the derivative, and even less inclination to disguise that impatience

title: Fire breaks out at commercial complex in Ahmedabad; 25 persons rescued
content: The blaze apparently originated near an ATM facility inside the private bank branch on the ground floor and quickly intensified, spreading to the parking area and higher levels, according to officials

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Brisk polling, a boycott, and a beachside standoff in Mayiladuthurai and Nagapattinam districts
content: Residents of Muthalaimedu Thittu, a hamlet under Sirkazhi Assembly constituency, boycotted polling to protest the lack of road access and drinking water for years.

title: Supreme Court issues notice on plea of Enrica Lexie owner for return of ₹3 cr bond
content: Supreme Court order came on the basis of a decision by the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS) in May 2020 to have the duo stand trial in Italy

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Tiruchi district clocks heavy turnout amid peaceful voting
content: Brisk polling despite the blistering summer in rural pockets all through the day with tight security arrangements in place; a couple of EVMs develop glitches in Manachanallur

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Torn fisherfolk families in Thangachimadam turn to polls as last resort
content: Conviction that the ballots remained a vital tool for ‘freedom’ of fishmen families is what ultimately compell the villagers to head to the polling stations

title: K.V. Thomas sees LDF retaining power in Kerala, calls Congress infighting ‘pitiable’
content: Kerala’s Special Representative in New Delhi says ‘people are not against the government but are desirous of change, which has always been the case. At the same time, they want continuity in governance and expect all current development works to move forward under those who initiated them’

title: ZPM secures landslide victory in Aizawl Municipal Corporation polls
content: Zoram People's Movement secures victory in Aizawl Municipal Corporation polls, bagging 17 seats in the 19-member civic body, officials said

title: Mood of Bengal suggests BJP victory in polls, will return during oath-taking ceremony: PM Modi
content: Mr. Modi attacked the ruling Trinamool Congress, accusing it of presiding over ‘syndicate raj’, political violence and economic decline

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Over 84% voter turnout in the State, highest in its electoral history
content: Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar said that it was the highest percentage of polling in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu. “[The] ECI salutes each voter of West Bengal and Tamil Nadu,” he said

title: Overfed and undernourished in Delhi
content: India’s capital faces what public health professionals call the Double Burden of Malnutrition, a condition in which children over-consume calories that lack essential nutrients. Combined with the lack of physical activity, the addictive nature of junk food, and parents unaware of nutritional realities, this early undernutrition is a risk factor for obesity later in life. A report on Delhi’s obesity-without-nutrition problem that needs immediate attention

title: ED cracks down on suspected interstate liquor racket, raids 9 sites in Arunachal
content: Officials said liquor designated for sale within Arunachal Pradesh was allegedly diverted and supplied illegally to markets in Assam and other States

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Accessible polls remain out of reach for persons with disabilities
content: As most polling stations were situated in schools, the grounds were filled with sand or gravel, making it unsuitable for wheelchairs to navigate

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Impressive turnout in central districts after SIR
content: A striking feature was the turnout of voters in urban segments where polling is  usually sluggish in the initial hours; actor Vijay, who is contesting in Tiruchi East, seem to inspired young first-time voters to turn up at the booth

title: Kerala’s power crisis due to West Asia conflict, says Electricity Minister Krishnankutty
content: Minister says shortage of cooking gas has led to increased reliance on induction cookers, significantly pushing up power usage across Kerala

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Amid sweltering heat, polling staff struggle with lack of ventilation, well-maintained toilets
content: From stinky toilets to inadequate ventilation, polling staff continued their work amid the poorly maintained conditions at some of the polling centres in south Chennai

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Brisk polling witnessed in Virudhunagar district
content: The day went off peacefully without any untoward incident, says Superintendent of Police N. Shreenatha

title: "Historic Decision": Ladakh Adds 5 New Districts To Boost Remote Governance
content: For residents of far-flung villages tucked in the Himalayas, this promises something tangible: offices closer home, faster paperwork, and services that no longer require days of travel.

title: No Supreme Court Relief For Gurugram Builder Floors Amid Demolition Drive
content: The demolition drive is part of an anti-encroachment exercise planned by the Haryana Shahari Vikas Pradhikaran (HSVP), which is set to cover 44 sectors of Gurugram starting today

title: Prominent J&K Madrasa Declared Unlawful Entity Over Jamaat-E-Islami Links
content: The order says that individuals affiliated with the Jamaat-e-Islami exercise their influence over administrative and academic matters of the seminary.

title: Delhi Sizzles With Temperature Over 40 Degrees. Will It Rain Tomorrow?
content: The maximum temperature may reach 44 degrees today in Delhi and NCR, but relief might just be on its way.

title: 4 Of Mumbai Family Die Of Suspected Food Poisoning After Eating Biryani, Watermelon
content: Mumbai, April 27 (IANS) Four members of a family, a 40-year-old husband, his 35-year-old wife, and their two daughters aged 16 and 13 have died due to suspected food poisoning in Mumbai’s Pydhonie area, police said on Monday.

title: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran Blames US For Collapse Of Talks As Minister Arrives In Russia
content: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian told Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif during a phone call Saturday night that the US "should first remove operational obstacles, including the blockade"

title: India, New Zealand Ink 'Once-In-A-Generation' Pact: All You Need To Know
content: India-New Zealand Free Trade Agreement: The pact aims to double bilateral trade to about $5 billion over the next five years.

title: CIA Agents Killed In Mexico Crash Weren't Authorised To Operate On Its Soil
content: Two US officials who are believed to have been members of the CIA were killed in an accident in the northern state of Chihuahua, Mexico.

title: Oracle To Cut Over 20,000 Jobs: Is AI Hitting Senior Employees First?
content: The cuts at Oracle are not an isolated event. Over the past month alone, companies including Meta, Microsoft, Disney and ASML have announced layoffs.

title: Trinamool MP Alleges Attack On Car By BJP Workers, Poll Body Seeks Report
content: Trinamool Congress MP Mitali Bag alleges BJP workers vandalised her car during campaign in Hooghly district

title: Iran's 'Quadruple' Warning To Gulf Nations After Trump's Threats
content: The comments came after US President Donald Trump indicated that sustained pressure could disrupt Iran's ability to transfer oil to storage or tankers.

title: 6 Snakebite Deaths In A Week In Kerala, Experts Blame Summer Heat
content: Between April 20 and April 26, snakebite deaths were reported from six districts across Kerala, making it the deadliest week of the season so far.

title: As Some Hijabs Come Off In Iran, Restrictions Still In Place
content: Images of bareheaded women sipping coffee in cafes in Tehran, in apparent defiance of the Islamic republic's strict dress rule, have stirred interest outside Iran -- but for...

title: China's DeepSeek Slashes Prices For New AI Model
content: China's DeepSeek is offering developers a 75% discount on its newly unveiled AI model, DeepSeekV4Pro, until May 5. The company is also cutting prices for input cache hits across

title: Iran School Textbooks Call US "Great Satan", Include Missile Math: Report
content: The books were published last year following a 12-day heightened conflict between Iran, Israel and the US.

title: Iran Offers To End Hormuz Chokehold Without Nuclear Deal
content: Trump has questioned who is in charge in Iran at the moment and said confusion within its theocracy made it difficult to reach a deal.

title: Opinion: Opinion | A '45%' Problem: The Troubling Reason Trump Is Avoiding Another Strike On Iran
content: America has depleted nearly half of its key missile stockpiles in the Iran war, and replacing these systems will take three to five years, even with expanded production contracts already signed.

title: At 46.9 Degrees, This Maharashtra City Is The Hottest In India
content: On Sunday, Akola recorded 46.9 degrees Celsius, the highest temperature in the country, followed by Amravati at 46.8 degrees Celsius.

title: Isro plans to send civilians with STEM backgrounds to space
content: Chethan Kumar is a Senior Assistant Editor with the Times of India. Aside from specialising in Space & Science, he has reported extensively on varied topics, with special focus on defence, policy and data stories. He has covered multiple elections, too. As a young democracy grows out of adolescence, Chethan feels, there are reels of tales emerging which need to be captured. To do this, he alternates between the mundane goings-on of the Common Man and the wonder-filled worlds of scientists and scamsters, politicians and soldiers. In a career spanning nearly 18 years, he has reported from multiple datelines — Houston, Florida, Kochi, Hyderabad, Chennai, Sriharikota (AP), NH-1 (J&K Highway), New Delhi, Ahmedabad, Raichur, Bhatkal, Mysuru, Chamarajanagar, to name a few —  but is based out of Bengaluru, India’s science capital that also hosts the ISRO HQ. Read More

title: Iran's desperate move: Tehran revives 'ghost ship' to store crude as US blocks export
content: the TOI Business Desk is a vigilant and dedicated team of journalists committed to delivering the latest and most relevant business news from around the world to readers of the Times of India . the desk ensures that TOI readers are well-informed about the ever-changing and dynamic world of commerce .

title: Phantom gas leak discovered in the ocean: Scientists warn of an invisible climate threat
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives.
